coreClaim: Messages submitted from Deki and conversation inputs continue through user-message persistence even when the originating UI surface navigates away or stops observing the send.

inScope: Focused regression proof for detached Deki sends

claimBoundary.coreClaim: Deki send work is durable after UI unmount, and conversation draft restoration only happens when the user message was not accepted.
entrypoints: DekiSurface send button and Enter shortcut
ConversationInput send button and Enter shortcut
currentPathsOrFormats: Deki session history through dekiApi.history append/saveCompaction
outOfScopeClaims: No browser navigation recording was captured in this PR.
No changes to provider request transport or Rust command routing.

contractLaneGate.brokenContract: src/features Deki UI owned the full async send chain, so unmount/navigation could remove the observer before persisted completion was reflected back into the session UI.
contractLaneGate.impliedContract: Once a send starts, user-message persistence and assistant persistence continue independently from component mount state; UI callbacks are observers, not owners.
contractLaneGate.actualEnforcement: runDetachedDekiSend performs append, compaction, Deki prompt, and assistant append before returning; DekiSurface callbacks check mountedRef before local state writes; ConversationInput tracks onUserMessageAccepted before restoring drafts.
contractLaneGate.primaryOwnerDetail: Feature-level orchestration around existing engine Deki contracts and shared API wrappers.
contractLaneGate.wrongLaneFixToAvoid: Do not move this into src-tauri or src/engine because the bug is feature UI lifecycle ownership, not native persistence or product generation rules.
regressionProof: src/features/shell/deki/lib/deki-send.spec.ts persists the user and assistant messages without UI observers
pnpm check validates architecture and full repo gates

positiveRows.claim: Detached Deki send persists both user and assistant messages without UI observers.
Existing generation UI behavior remains intact around stream flushing and page visibility.
The PR preserves repo architecture boundaries and broad validation gates.
positiveRows.evidence: pnpm check passed after the final diff, including check:architecture, check:frontend, check:rust, check:docs, check:agent-workflow, and warning-only unused-code reporting.

contradictionRows.claim: Draft restoration should not run after the backend accepted the user message.
contradictionRows.evidence: ConversationInput now sets userMessageAccepted from onUserMessageAccepted and only calls restoreSubmittedDraft when it remains false.

contradictionRows.claim: Unmounted Deki UI must not receive local setState calls after detached work completes.
contradictionRows.evidence: DekiSurface wraps local message/compaction/sending/error writes from send completion callbacks in mountedRef checks.

untestedRows: No browser navigation recording was captured; command proof covers the durable helper and shared generation regression surface.

ownedFacts.description: Deki assistant messages on mainline carry response.action into dekiApi.history.appendMessage, and the detached runner preserves that action.
ownedFacts.evidence: src/shared/api/deki-api.ts appendMessage accepts action; src/features/shell/deki/lib/deki-send.ts passes response.action.

manualBlockers.reason: The core persistence invariant is covered by the detached send unit test and full local gate; browser recording would be additional UX evidence.

edgeRowsTested: Deki detached send without UI observers
Conversation accepted-message callback before draft restoration
negativeRowsTested: Conversation draft restore is suppressed once userMessageAccepted is true

pr.checks: local pnpm check passed; CI pending at ledger creation
